channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Aid cuts, drought and conflict leave Somalis desperate
item.description: Drought, conflict and aid cuts push Somalis into hunger as Kismayo IDP camps struggle to cope with the crisis.

item.title: Wembanyama and Spurs rebound to level against Timberwolves in NBA Playoffs
item.description: Castle's 21 points and Wembanyama's 19 helped Spurs crush T'wolves 133-95 as Knicks take 2-0 lead over 76ers.

item.title: Israeli attack on Gaza kills one person, wounds son of Hamas’s al-Hayya
item.description: Khalil al-Hayya says his son, Azzam, was 'seriously wounded' in deadly Israeli attack on Gaza City.

item.title: Toronto World Cup tickets to be resold for face value on FIFA Marketplace
item.description: Tickets for matches at other venues may be listed above face value on the official resale and exchange marketplace.

item.title: What’s the full match schedule, groups and format for World Cup 2026?
item.description: All the teams, groups, key dates and kickoff times for the FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States.

item.title: Bolivian police fire tear gas at protesting teachers
item.description: Bolivian police fired tear gas at protesters in La Paz, as three separate groups rallied against the government.

item.title: Photo shows Israeli soldier desecrating Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
item.description: Israel's military launches a probe after photo shows soldier placing a cigarette into the mouth of Virgin Mary statue.

item.title: France introduces one‑euro canteen meals for all students
item.description: French universities are now offering 1 euro meals to all students, regardless of income.

item.title: Criminal charges in Bosnian mine lead poisoning
item.description: A Canadian mining group is facing a criminal complaint after hundreds tested positive for lead poisoning in Bosnia.

item.title: South Korean court reduces Han Duck-soo’s prison term in martial law case
item.description: Seoul appeals court cuts Han Duck-soo's prison sentence from 23 years to 15.

item.title: Israeli soldier caught desecrating statue of Virgin Mary in Lebanon
item.description: Outrage is growing over a photo of an Israeli soldier pushing a cigarette into the mouth of a statue of the Virgin Mary.

item.title: BTS draws 50,000 fans in less than five hours to Mexico City palace
item.description: K-pop group BTS appeared on the balcony of Mexico’s National Palace after meeting President Claudia Sheinbaum.

item.title: US says Hezbollah is ‘trying to derail talks’ with Israel
item.description: US State Department spokesperson says Hezbollah is ‘trying to derail talks’ with Israel.

item.title: Rising Fuel costs overshadowing agenda for ASEAN summit in the Philippines
item.description: ASEAN leaders have begun meeting in the Philippines as residents say their main concern is living costs.

item.title: North Korea says it is not bound by any treaty on nuclear non-proliferation
item.description: Pyongyang says its status as nuclear-armed state 'will not change based on external rhetorical claims'.

item.title: Police arrest protester after five-days atop Washington bridge
item.description: Police have arrested an anti-war protester after he ended a five-day sit-in atop Washington’s Frederick Douglass bridge.

item.title: Iran war live: Trump says deal with Tehran ‘possible’; Israel bombs Beirut
item.description: UN calls on Israel to free two members of Gaza aid flotilla abducted in international waters and held without charge.

item.title: What are China’s surprise gains in the war on Iran?
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is in Beijing, seeking support on a deal with the US.

item.title: Israeli strikes kill five Palestinians across Gaza in one day
item.description: Five Palestinians were killed in one day in separate Israeli strikes across Gaza.

item.title: Argentina investigates link to deadly hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
item.description: Three deaths reported from South America-related hantavirus as cruise ship remains off Cape Verde coast.

item.title: In rare push, US lawmakers demand transparency on Israel nuclear capability
item.description: Lawmakers say US policy of ambiguity on Israel's nuclear capabilities heightens risks amid US-Israel war on Iran.

item.title: Russia tells diplomats to leave Kyiv in case Moscow launches mass strikes
item.description: Moscow threatens retaliatory strikes if Ukraine attacks Russia during Victory Day commemorations.

item.title: SpaceX backs Anthropic with data centre deal amidst Musk’s OpenAI lawsuit
item.description: Deal gives IPO-bound SpaceX a marquee customer, while helping Anthropic ease capacity constraints.

item.title: Trump says war will be ‘over quickly’ as Iran reviews US peace proposal
item.description: US president indicates momentum building for a deal, but Iran says it is still reviewing Washington's proposal.

item.title: Holders PSG beat Bayern Munich to reach Champions League final with Arsenal
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain draw the second leg of their semifinal with Bayern Munich 1-1 in Germany to take the tie 6-4.
